steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))
CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: The pancreas is poorly seen due to overlying bowel gas; however, no focal fluid collection is seen about the imaged portions of the pancreas.

alarm_detect.summary: The right upper quadrant ultrasound explicitly states the pancreas was 'poorly seen due to overlying bowel gas,' rendering it non-diagnostic for the pancreas — the organ most relevant to the markedly elevated lipase (734 IU/L) and amylase (338 IU/L), so the imaging question about pancreatic pathology remains unanswered.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is the appropriate modality to directly visualize the pancreas and characterize pancreatitis severity, explicitly ordered because the ultrasound failed to image the pancreas due to bowel gas — precisely the study inadequacy flagged.
